Hcb.metaDescription: Start your nonprofit with HCB: a 501(c)(3) legal entity, bank account, automatic taxes and accounting, and best-in-class software.
Hcb.heroBody: Start your nonprofit with our fiscal sponsorship program, HCB: a 501(c)(3) legal entity, bank account, automatic taxes & accounting, and best-in-class software.
Hcb.mobileAlertBody: Manage your HCB organizations on the go. Issue cards, view transactions, and more!
Hcb.pocketBody: The official mobile app lets you manage your organization's finances, issue cards, and more!
Hcb.pocketFeatureSpendingBody: Stay up to date on your organization's balance and transactions.
Hcb.pocketFeatureTapBody: No extra hardware required! Tap any card against your phone.
Hcb.pocketFeatureCardsBody: Add cards to Apple Wallet and Google Wallet directly.
Hcb.pocketFeatureReceiptsBody: Quickly snap a photo or upload a file!
Hcb.feesBody: This fee goes directly to Hack Club's operations staff, including teen interns working under mentors. This allows us to deliver best-in-class software and support, grow sustainably, while also providing paid career training for young people from diverse backgrounds.
Hcb.builtByCollageAlt: Thousands of teenagers attend Hack Club events like this
Hcb.builtByP1: As <home>Hack Club</home> grew, we needed a way to empower our members. We currently have over 60,000 high schoolers involved in Hack Club with over 400 clubs around the world.
Hcb.builtByP2: We started HCB in 2018 to support teen-led clubs and hackathons. After showing it to our educational partners, we knew we had tapped into something much larger. Today, HCB removes financial and legal barriers for thousands doing good in their community.
Hcb.builtByEnvironment: As part of our commitment to the environment, funding for HCB's operations and staff will never come from the <fossil>fossil fuel industry</fossil>.
Hcb.openSourceTitle: Open source infrastructure for fiscally sponsored organizations.
Hcb.openSourceBody: HCB is open source and built in public, like many other Hack Club projects. Join us in building the infrastructure powering fiscally sponsored organizations around the world.
Hcb.gourceAlt: HCB commit history visualised with Gource
Hcb.contactNotice: HCB Visa® Commercial cards are powered by Stripe and issued by Celtic Bank.

HcbFirst.metaTitle: Financial Toolkit for FIRST Teams | HCB
HcbFirst.metaDescription: HCB is the ultimate booster club for FRC, FTC, and FLL teams to receive grants, fundraise, make purchases, and much more.
HcbFirst.heroTitle: The ultimate booster club for <highlight>FRC, FTC, and FLL teams</highlight>.
HcbFirst.heroBody: Built by <first>FIRST</first> alumni for <first>FIRST</first> teams, HCB is a comprehensive financial platform used by hundreds of clubs, teams and hackathons.
HcbFirst.featuresBody:
Organize your team's finances in real time, receive grants, gain nonprofit status, & more.

Use features engineered by FIRST alumni to help you run a successful team.
HcbFirst.dashboardAlt: HCB dashboard shown on a device
HcbFirst.featureNonprofitBody: Become part of Hack Club's legal entity, getting the benefits of our 501(c)(3) tax status.
HcbFirst.featureBalanceBody: Keep everyone on your team and beyond up to date with real-time balance and transaction history.
HcbFirst.featureCardsBody: Issue physical debit cards to all your teammates.
HcbFirst.featureGrantsBody: Easily receive and deposit money from grants and donations into your account. You'll also get a customizable online donation form to share with friends and family.
HcbFirst.featureReimburseBody: Reimburse teammates for expenses with flexible money transfer options including ACH, mailed checks, and more.
HcbFirst.featureSupportBody: With 24-hour response time on weekdays, we'll never leave you hanging.
HcbFirst.testimonialsTitle: <first>FIRST</first> teams all over the country run on HCB.
HcbFirst.testimonialsBody: Everywhere from San Jose to Boston to New York, HCB powers teams of all sizes.
HcbFirst.applyBody: Open to Hack Clubs, hackathons, and charitable organizations in the US and Canada.
HcbFirst.runHq: We run Hack Club HQ on HCB!

HcbMobile.metaTitle: HCB Mobile is here! — Hack Club
HcbMobile.metaDescription: Manage your HCB organizations on the go. Issue cards, view transactions, and more!
HcbMobile.heroSubtitle: Manage your HCB organizations on the go. Issue cards, view transactions, and more!
HcbMobile.p1: I'm Mohamad, a 17-year-old from the SF Bay Area, and I just shipped the official mobile app for HCB.
HcbMobile.p2: If you haven't heard of it, HCB is the financial backbone for over 6,500 teenager-led nonprofits, clubs, and hackathons. We provide 501(c)(3) nonprofit status, access to a bank account, a donation collection platform, and debit cards for thousands of young people trying to do good in their communities.
HcbMobile.p3: HCB is currently processing an average of $6 million per month (over $80M in its lifetime)1. For the last year, I've led the project to build the first-ever mobile app for this entire community.
HcbMobile.p4: The entire project is open source on GitHub (we'd love a ⭐️!).
HcbMobile.whyBody: These teenagers are running robotics teams, hackathons, and nonprofit projects that improve their community. They need a way to manage their organization's finances from their pocket. With HCB Mobile, they'll be able to:
HcbMobile.whyLi1: Track their organization's balance and transactions on the go.
HcbMobile.whyLi2: Accept in-person tap-to-pay donations, perfect for an in-person fundraiser or event! No extra hardware required. Just tap any credit/debit card against your phone.
HcbMobile.whyLi3: Issue new debit cards, add them to Apple / Google Wallet, and freeze or cancel them directly from their phone.
HcbMobile.whyLi4: Upload receipts directly from their device or match existing receipts in Receipt Bin to transactions with a tap.
HcbMobile.techP1: When I started working on this app, I wanted to build in native code like SwiftUI for iOS and Kotlin/Jetpack Compose for Android. However, I realized that it would be a pain for me, a full-time student with classes, to handle two codebases. I'd have to duplicate every feature I created for one OS to the other and deal with all the integration issues along the way. Then, I discovered Expo (a React Native framework) which allowed me to write one app that worked on multiple devices. Working with Expo, I learned about creating my own Expo Modules (to bridge native code features to Typescript) and optimization methods like memoization and component recycling.
HcbMobile.techP2: The non-code side of this app was no joke, either. I had to work with the Apple and Google app review teams to obtain restricted entitlements for features like mobile tap-to-pay terminal provisioning (made possible by Stripe) and push provisioning (which allows users to add cards to their payment wallet directly from HCB Mobile). It took several months and many back-and-forth email chains to finally get the entitlements we needed.
HcbMobile.techP3: After over 250 hours of development work, I can say that I'm incredibly proud of HCB Mobile because it's built by teenagers to make it easier for teenagers like me to run nonprofit organizations and projects with HCB. Beyond teenagers, HCB also supports hundreds of adult-run organizations such as mutual aid groups, open source projects, and community spaces.
HcbMobile.appStoreAria: Download HCB on the App Store
HcbMobile.footnote: This amount is excluding HQ (our own) <finances>finances</finances>.
HcbMobile.ctaBody: We're accepting applications! No startup fees, no minimum balance, and no long wait time.

HcbOpenSource.metaTitle: HCB is now open source! | Hack Club
HcbOpenSource.metaDescription: Our fiscal sponsorship platform's codebase is now publicly available under the AGPL license and we're continuing to encourage transparency amongst nonprofits.
HcbOpenSource.heroSubtitle: Our fiscal sponsorship platform's <codebase>codebase</codebase> is now publicly available under the AGPL license and we're continuing to encourage transparency amongst nonprofits.
HcbOpenSource.p1: Hack Club launched HCB in 2018 to enable hackathons to raise and spend money through <fs>fiscal sponsorship</fs>. Since then, we've expanded to all nonprofit projects; our 12,000 users have transacted $50 million.
HcbOpenSource.uiAlt: HCB's user interface showing Hack Club HQ's transactions
HcbOpenSource.p2: Local student-run hackathons, robotics teams, and community groups use HCB as a nonprofit neobank to collect donations, send payments, issue debit cards, and gain 501(c)(3) status.
HcbOpenSource.p3: When we started HCB, it was developed in private for security reasons. That said, one of Hack Club's core principles has always been transparency — we <opensource>open source</opensource> our <finances>finances</finances>, <events>document how we run events</events>, and have 500+ public repositories on <github>GitHub</github>.
HcbOpenSource.p4: github.com/hackclub/hcb is now public — check it out and star it.
HcbOpenSource.p5: Paired with our technical documentation, it's a great resource for anyone interested in building financial software or applications with Ruby on Rails. Our engineering work is also entirely public; the world can learn from our successes and mistakes.
HcbOpenSource.p6: Since 2018, over fifty people have made 10k+ commits to HCB (thank you!); we can't wait for more contributors to join us:
HcbOpenSource.videoFallback: Your browser does not support the video tag.
HcbOpenSource.ps: PS: if you're looking to start a nonprofit, we're accepting applications! Head over to <nonprofit>nonprofit.new</nonprofit> and we'll be in touch.
HcbOpenSource.photoAlt: Hack Club team launching the announcement live from San Francisco
HcbOpenSource.photoCaption: We're launching this live from SF!
HcbOpenSource.welcomeBody: Hack Club is a global community of high school makers & student-led coding clubs. We've got a 24/7 Slack chatroom of 104k+ teenagers learning to code & building amazing projects, & you'll fit right in.

HcbFeatures.headline: Powerful financial tools built by our nonprofit, for yours.
HcbFeatures.subtext: Since day one, we've built beautiful, self-serve software to empower you to raise and spend money without administrative hassle. We're also open source!
HcbFeatures.openSourceGithub: See our open source on GitHub
